message: Investigated web_read issue: root cause is pi-search-hub web_read defaulting to anonymous Jina Reader; direct r.jina.ai and s.jina.ai calls return 401 AuthenticationRequiredError for AS7922. SAF is to configure a credentialed/verified reader at Pi search extension layer, not patch Subagent007.

Implemented no-secret web_read SAF in local Pi runtime: upgraded pi-search-hub to 2.8.0, set global reader to firecrawl, clarified web_read reader guidance, verified Subagent007 web_read returns Firecrawl results for example.com and npm registry.

Implemented three SAF repairs: parent-exit/process-group cleanup for fake child orphans; provider usage-limit reset/retry metadata propagation into results and failure logs; opt-in local active-child launch fuse with preflight rejection and no queue. Verified with typecheck, docs check, targeted tests, full npm test.

Implementation-tightener pass: removed unused active-child lease enabled flag and closed a promoted-run lease release gap if auto-promotion status persistence fails before the async child run is installed. Rebuilt and verified with typecheck, focused SAF tests, and full npm test.

Ran implementation-tightener pass over current child-capacity/usage-limit/process-cleanup diff. Typecheck passed; focused tests passed for usage-limit metadata, local child fuse, heartbeat cleanup, and parent-exit child-group cleanup. No behavior-preserving tightening applied.

Ran simplifier loop on current dirty diff. Reset .git/simplifier state, recorded npm test green baseline (191 pass), recorded scan map and two zero-admissible scans, generated .git/simplifier/REPORT.md. No findings or patches; finalize blocked only because worktree remains dirty/user-owned.

Simplifier loop finalized for SAF repairs: one low-risk lease-release duplication candidate was validated, full npm test passed 191/191, remaining capacity/cleanup/provider metadata code is load-bearing boundary behavior.

Cococo-cleaner pass reconciled local project-memory scaffold with source reality: documented package scripts/setup, removed broken pattern-index examples, and populated architecture/stack/conventions/decisions/router with verified Subagent007 Pi facts. Source behavior unchanged.
README clarified durable-run operation tools and local validation gates after verifying docs:check remains green.
README-fix documented provider usage-limit reset/retry metadata and primary source boundaries for agent readers. Verified with docs:check, diff check, and mex check.

Observed customer campaign SAF repaired public semantic failure projection: run-operation ValidationErrors now return operation_rejected instead of MCP text errors, required packet failures distinguish not-ready from invalid, and observed campaign oracles enforce the typed results. Verified with typecheck, focused tests, repaired full-current campaign, runtime readiness, docs:check, and npm test 191/191.

Implementation-tightener pass narrowed the observed probe result class from generic packet_failed to packet_required_not_ready so campaign coverage asserts the exact packet taxonomy repair instead of a loose packet-failure bucket. Verified with observed-campaign test, typecheck, docs:check, diff check, and mex check.

Simplifier SIM-001 removed operationRejectedResult's unreachable fallback reason-code computation; withRunFailureLogging remains the single reason-code computation point for operation rejection logging and structured MCP results. Static, targeted, and full source oracles passed.
Public prompt text is now projected to a redacted marker in public run views/transcript provenance, and observed campaigns assert prompt sentinels are absent from public artifacts, not only ledgers.
Tightened observed tool discovery coverage so campaigns assert required public tools and skill alias guidance instead of generic listTools success.
Observed tool-listing coverage now treats unexpected public MCP tools as a caller-visible defect, so discovery proves the exact public surface rather than only required-tool presence.

Public model calibration is class-only: MCP results, failure logs, session artifacts, README, and observed-campaign summaries must not expose concrete model IDs or thinking levels; observed probes now assert forbidden calibration fields are absent.

Observed campaign found missing named-session require_existing was reported as a durable failed run despite no child launch; repaired by adding read-only manifest eligibility preflight before task registration while leaving locked session execution checks as race protection.

Simplifier SIM-001: inlined the single-use validateSessionManifestPreflight helper into validateRunSubagentSessionRequestPreflight; validation order and MCP preflight rejection contract stay unchanged.

Observed campaign SAF repair: removed public/session thinking-level calibration fields and broadened scoped negative oracles so future public thinking_level field variants fail while private legacy parsing remains supported.

Simplifier loop finalized after the observed-campaign SAF repair; no further admissible behavior-preserving simplifications found because public/private artifact exclusions, broad thinking_level field-name checks, and legacy manifest fallback paths are load-bearing boundary or compatibility behavior.
Cococo cleanup clarified public calibration wording to distinguish forbidden thinking-level calibration values from legitimate README mentions of rejected or legacy thinking_level field names.

Recursive Subagent007 calls are not blocked by source-level depth logic, but they are not transparent by default: children see Pi session tools, not parent Codex MCP tools. Live probe 2026-07-07 showed outer child had no Subagent007 launch tool because ~/.pi/agent packages/extensions omit Subagent007; recursion would require registering Subagent007 in Pi and then managing local capacity/timeouts.

Recursive Subagent007 delegation is parent-owned: children receive a native delegate tool backed by private local IPC, while the server schedules descendants as durable runs with lineage/depth metadata and a bounded recursion depth.
Recursive delegate lineage is now derived from active parent run state in runTask.ts; IPC caller lineage is treated as a claim to validate, not an authority to schedule descendants.
Final-mode Subagent007 runs now require a captured final message; clean child exits without final output fail generically as missing_final_output rather than using skill-specific finalization checks.
Cococo cleanup narrowed session failure reason_code projections to the shared FailureReasonCode type after the missing-final-output implementation.

Observed full-current campaign now covers recursive delegate success lineage, max-depth rejection, and forged-lineage rejection with strict run-view evidence and private recursive-control leakage checks; production recursive runtime was unchanged because targeted trials and existing runtime tests already passed.

Cococo cleaner stabilized test JSONL reads because aggregate runs can observe terminal in-memory run state before failure-log files are visible on disk; production failure logging still awaits writes during finalization.

Observed recursive trials found parent run views only exposed child_run_ids, not recursive child lifecycle events. Repaired by projecting sanitized recursive_child_started/recursive_child_finished parent events and adding wait-0 descendant completion coverage.

Expanded full-current observed campaign coverage to require missing-final-output, named-session require_existing missing-session preflight, and active-child capacity exhaustion/release after observed customer-style campaign found these documented edge contracts were only covered by ad hoc trials.

README observed-campaign guidance now names the full-current edge surfaces required by the manifest: missing-final-output, session require_existing preflight, and active-child capacity exhaustion/release, so future agents choose the right probe profile.
Session packet-failure telemetry now preserves durable caller context and full-current observed coverage includes start_session_run packet failures.
Centralized skill binding, made validation reasonCode the failure-log authority, removed observed lifecycle_phases coverage metadata, and made legacy tool_profile input-only compatibility.
Added opt-in Subagent007 v2 live input with receipt metadata and no durable raw answer; Bendum bridge remains the next integration slice.

Replaced dual caller-input protocols with durable-run contract v2: receipts now require child waiter acceptance, replay comparison is live-memory only, and run-owned ordering resolves answer/cancel/finalization races without persisting raw answers.
Updated A-E internal model calibration and upgraded bundled Pi coding-agent to 0.80.6 so the project-local runtime can resolve every calibrated model.

Cancellation eligibility belongs at the Subagent007 caller contract and mandatory reviewer gate: working/running_silent, elapsed silence, live heartbeats, and recursive child activity never authorize cancellation; only explicit user intent or a real caller-owned stop condition does.
Adjusted class D thinking calibration from xhigh to high while keeping openai-codex/gpt-5.6-terra.
Terminal snapshots now own compacted event and input views; named-session attempts and suite-owned test state are cleaned by their lifecycle owners.
Document Pi runtime accurately: normal MCP execution uses bundled @earendil-works/pi dependencies; only model inventory reconciliation requires a separate pi CLI.

Replace capped transcript rendering and unbounded raw spools with complete sanitized streaming transcripts, owned lifecycle cleanup, disk-reserve fail-closed behavior, finite child capacity, restart-safe partial recovery, and atomic build publication.

Implementation tightening removed obsolete whole-buffer transcript rendering and unused state/config, narrowed owner and lease records to enforced facts, retained active-child leases through durable terminal persistence, and made transcript recovery idempotent across atomic publication.

Audited README against package scripts, runtime constants, environment keys, model reconciliation behavior, and durable-run contract; removed repeated tool/skill/cancellation/campaign guidance and corrected the standalone Pi CLI from required to optional inventory input.

Cococo cleanup removed dead knip configuration and a stale generated frozen runtime tree, then reconciled active-child, Pi CLI, capacity-owner, and disk-reserve documentation without changing runtime behavior.

Raised the shared default active-child launch fuse from 8 to 24 to support parallel workflows while retaining a configurable host-level runaway-launch guard; explicit overrides and zero opt-out remain unchanged.

Added bounded owner-scoped admission queueing for top-level start_run and schedule_run: 24 active children, 96 queued metadata-only tickets by default, queued cancellation and promotion, launch-time safety rechecks, no prompt persistence, and fail-fast session/one-shot/recursive behavior to avoid contract drift and recursive deadlock.

Tightened bounded admission queue ownership: one process-owned pump replaces per-ticket directory polling; lease/ticket records publish atomically and unreadable records fail closed; processRunner owns child_started at actual spawn; terminal capacity is released only after confirmed lease deletion.
Implemented owner-bounded garbage hygiene without TTL deletion of caller-owned outputs or sessions.
Tightened failure telemetry: one locked append/archive owner, atomic lock publication, and bounded off-response worker.
Cococo cleanup reconciled queue/setup memory, restored the observed-campaign build dependency, and removed private failure-worker job identity waste.

Observed customer campaign: expanded deterministic full-current coverage to recursive two-hop/depth-boundary, session resume, queue lifecycle, and exact input retry; removed backend session IDs/mailbox paths from public MCP projections and fixed named-session child-start propagation.

Tightened public result projection and observed campaign oracles: all object responses use the privacy projector; queue coverage now proves promotion before queued cancellation; recursive checks require every link and no excess descendant.
Clarified README observed-trial guidance: full-current is deterministic coverage; installed Pi uses live-current with the fake child override unset and remains integration smoke only.
Removed unused generic .mex scaffold templates; the root AGENTS.md and populated .mex context are the sole project guidance.
Observed full-current and normal-Pi live-current campaigns found no caller-visible defects; retained zero-change plan with provider/model variability explicitly residual.

SAF-1 repair: handler preflight retry guidance now selects timeout-underbudget advice from ValidationError.reasonCode and no longer parses error text; verified build, typecheck, focused MCP tests, full suite, docs, and runtime readiness.
README audit: removed duplicate tool_profile compatibility statement; input contract remains the source of that rule.
Cococo cleanup: marked the acknowledged-input plan as historical and clarified its stale present-tense implementation wording.
Clarified the intentional MCP SDK schema-error versus handler-level structured-preflight boundary for callers and maintainers; no runtime behavior changed.
Cococo cleanup reconciled four stale TODO statuses in the state-hygiene ledger with their verified trace and evidence; no runtime behavior changed.
Cococo cleanup removed an unreferenced generic patterns scaffold and repaired the router stale template link; concrete indexed patterns remain the only guidance.
Mapped class A to openai-codex/gpt-5.6-luna at runtime low (user-facing light) and class B to the same model at medium.

Make session locks definite-death only, recover candidate promotion through hash-verified pending commits, and expose unreadable legacy lease ambiguity as run_liveness_unknown rather than restart drift.

Implementation-tightener removed duplicate pending-commit authorities, derived staging identity from the committed record and manifest, and made new-format lease attribution prefix-free without changing public contracts.

T025 adds fail-closed workspace_read_only Pi construction allowlisting plus pre-prompt skill-content pins backed by run-owned snapshots; omitted effect_profile and legacy tool_profile remain unchanged.

T025 tightening: attest native request_input with the immutable JavaScript release digest while excluding mutable lease files, and accept activation only through the dedicated pre-prompt receipt event.

T025 digest clarification supersedes whole-release wording: native request_input identity covers its owning runtime modules and excludes unrelated release and live lease files; legacy Codex MCP registrations remain separate from Pi capabilities.

Added public version-1 batch skill-binding verification at the canonical Subagent007 resolver/hash boundary; verification is all-or-nothing, point-in-time, non-model, and operational-state-free, while launch retains its pre-prompt digest recheck.

Implementation-tightener removed one redundant back-to-back skill read/hash/compare inside runSubagentCore and reused the public result type in MCP integration tests; durable preflight and the launch-core recheck remain distinct.

Added authority-owned hashless skill resolution and explicit default-off recursive delegation with strict pre-prompt receipts, Pi registry enforcement, and terminal subtree closure; durable contract remains v2.

Implementation-tightener removed duplicate recursive authorization state and disabled-mode private-control construction, completed capability assertions, and reconciled public digest/session/tool-count documentation without changing the resolver or recursion contracts.

Canon Skill Smith owner foundation now binds deterministic complete runtime bundles to immutable owner snapshots, stable project/publication claims, retained active-or-closed references, exact deletion impact, and pre-prompt snapshot launch revalidation; exact-root validation supports both staging and canonical projection.

Added skill_creator_authoring_v1: a receipt-bound Pi construction allowlist of bounded task-root filesystem authoring tools with lexical/resolved path guards; platform .system skills now participate in canonical resolver ambiguity checks. Kept Researcher shell authority residual pending an exact controller capability.

Canon Skill Smith authoring now uses canonical path guards: read/grep/find/ls admit only the exact real task root plus the active revalidated complete snapshot runtime root; write/edit remain at the task root. Tests normalize macOS /var to /private/var mechanically and cover snapshot-sidecar dispatch and symlink rejection.

Admit canonical root license.txt into the complete runtime-bundle closure so platform Skill Creator bundles resolve and snapshot with the license bytes digested, while retaining strict root admission and existing safety limits.
Added standalone external expert model classes Z1 (Kimi K3), Z2 (Opus 4.8), and Z3 (GLM 5.2), all xhigh via OpenRouter; Kimi uses an explicit Pi transport fallback until native registry support.

Tightened external expert model classes: keep Z-to-concrete-model calibration internal on public README surfaces, resolve Kimi's explicit Pi transport fallback by provider/model reference rather than registry object identity, and document native-or-fallback registry compatibility.
Clarified that Z1-Z3 external expert classes require OpenRouter authentication in the Pi process and should be health-probed before one-shot reliance, while keeping concrete calibration internal.
